chapters.title: Execution Is a Discipline, Not a Slogan

summaryBullets: Treating execution as a repeatable leadership discipline rather than a motivational phrase creates clearer judgment.
Results improve when leaders stay close to the work that drives them.
This matters because strategy without operating discipline creates wishful thinking.
A common mistake is assuming a strong plan will execute itself once announced.
The pattern becomes easier to see when everyone agrees with the goal but nobody is closing the gap between plan and reality.
A practical starting move is to name the critical outcomes clearly, stay close to the operating details, and review progress against reality.
keyTakeaways: Treating execution as a repeatable leadership discipline rather than a motivational phrase creates clearer judgment. This perspective helps people make clearer decisions and follow through with more discipline when stakes rise.
practice: name the critical outcomes clearly
stay close to the operating details
review progress against reality
treat execution as part of leadership
